info.title: ReasonDB API
info.description:
A reasoning-native database API for AI agents. ReasonDB uses LLM-guided tree traversal for intelligent document search, optimized for AI agent workflows.

## Key Features

- **Hierarchical Document Storage**: Documents stored as navigable trees, not flat chunks
- **LLM-Guided Retrieval**: AI reasons through tree structure using summaries
- **Parallel Branch Exploration**: Concurrent traversal with beam search
- **Multi-Format Support**: PDFs, Markdown, HTML, and more
- **RQL Query Language**: SQL-like queries with SEARCH and REASON clauses

tags.name: tables
tags.description: Table (collection) management - organize documents into logical groups

tags.name: ingestion
tags.description: Document ingestion - import text, files, and URLs into hierarchical trees

tags.name: search
tags.description: LLM-guided search - intelligent tree traversal with reasoning

tags.name: query
tags.description: RQL queries - SQL-like queries with SEARCH and REASON clauses

tags.name: documents
tags.description: Document management - list, view, and delete documents

get.description: Returns OK if the server is running.

post.description: Creates a new table (collection) to organize documents. Documents can be assigned to tables during ingestion or moved later.

get.description: Returns a list of all tables in the database, including document counts.
Returns detailed information about a specific table.

patch.description: Update table metadata, name, or description. Only provided fields are updated.

delete.description: Delete a table. By default, documents in the table are moved to the default table. Set `cascade=true` in the request body to delete all documents in the table.

get.description: Returns all documents assigned to a specific table.

post.description: Ingest plain text or Markdown content directly. The content will be chunked, organized into a tree structure, and optionally summarized by an LLM. You must specify a table_id to assign the document to.
Upload a document file for ingestion. Supports PDF, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, images (with OCR), audio files (with transcription), HTML, and more via MarkItDown.

table_id.description: Table ID to assign the document to

post.description: Ingest content from a URL. Supports web pages, YouTube videos (with transcription), and other online resources. The content will be extracted, chunked, and organized into a tree.

Performs an intelligent search across documents using an LLM to navigate the hierarchical tree structure. The LLM evaluates each node's summary to decide which branches to explore, mimicking human reasoning. Returns relevant content with extracted answers and confidence scores.

Execute SQL-like queries against documents using ReasonDB Query Language (RQL).

Supports:
- `WHERE` clauses for filtering by tags, author, metadata
- `SEARCH` clause for BM25 full-text search (fast keyword matching)
- `REASON` clause for LLM semantic search (intelligent answer extraction)
- `GROUP BY` and aggregate functions (COUNT, SUM, AVG)
- `EXPLAIN` to view query execution plans

get.description: Returns a summary of all ingested documents in the database.
Returns full details for a specific document including metadata and tree info.

delete.description: Deletes a document and all its associated nodes (cascade delete).

get.summary: Get all nodes for a document
get.description: Returns a flat list of all nodes in the document tree.
Returns the complete hierarchical tree structure with nested children. Useful for visualizing or navigating the document structure.

cascade.description: If true, delete all documents in the table. If false, move them to default table.

document_count.description: Number of documents in this table

table_id.description: Table ID to assign the document to (REQUIRED - table must exist)

generate_summaries.description: Whether to generate LLM summaries (default: true)

url.description: URL to ingest (web page, YouTube video, etc.)

table_id.description: Table ID to assign the document to (REQUIRED - table must exist)

generate_summaries.description: Whether to generate LLM summaries (default: true)

total_nodes.description: Total number of nodes in the tree

query.description: The natural language query to search for
query.example: What are the key benefits of machine learning?

document_id.description: Optional document ID to search within (searches all if not provided)

table_id.description: Optional table ID to restrict search to

node_id.description: Node ID where content was found

path.description: Path from root to this node (breadcrumbs)

content.description: The relevant content at this node
content.example: Machine learning enables computers to learn from data...

answer.example: The key benefits include automation, pattern recognition, and predictive capabilities.

reasoning.description: LLM's reasoning for selecting this path
reasoning.example: This chapter covers ML fundamentals relevant to the query

nodes_pruned.description: Nodes pruned (not explored due to low confidence)

llm_calls.description: Number of LLM API calls made

query.example: SELECT * FROM legal WHERE author = 'Alice' LIMIT 10

score.description: Relevance score (BM25 for SEARCH, confidence for REASON)

confidence.description: Confidence score from LLM (for REASON queries)

step_type.description: Step type (e.g., TableScan, IndexScan, Filter, Aggregate)

description.description: Description of what this step does

root_node_id.description: Root node ID of the tree

summary.example: This chapter covers the fundamentals of neural networks...

is_leaf.description: Whether this is a leaf node (no children)

summary.example: This chapter covers neural network fundamentals...
